role: Minister of Foreign Affairs (2013–2021); Vice President (2024–2025)
bio: Mohammad Javad Zarif served as Iran's Foreign Minister throughout [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani)'s presidency, leading the Iranian negotiating team that concluded the [JCPOA](/events/jcpoa-nuclear-deal-2015) nuclear deal in 2015. A US-educated career diplomat, he was Iran's ambassador to the United Nations before becoming foreign minister. He endorsed [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian) in the 2024 presidential election and briefly served as vice president for strategic affairs before resigning. He was subject to US sanctions from 2019.
editorial_notes: Led JCPOA negotiations. Endorsed Pezeshkian in 2024.
